title: Malaxis langkawiensis
intro: Malaxis langkawiensis, also known as the Langkawi Malaxis, is a species of orchid endemic to Langkawi Island in Malaysia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its reported health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Bioactive constituents of Malaxis langkawiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include tea, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health issues in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Malaxis langkawiensis is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is classified within the genus Malaxis. Within the Orchidaceae family, Malaxis langkawiensis is further classified as a member of the subfamily Orchidoideae. The exact classification of Malaxis langkawiensis may vary based on taxonomic revisions, but it is generally recognized as a distinct species within the orchid family. More specific classification information may require consultation of recent botanical sources.
